cards.title: Aryabhata
cards.subtitle: The First Great Astronomer
cards.description: The 5th-century mathematician-astronomer whose Aryabhatiya gave a place-value system, an accurate value of pi, and the idea that the Earth rotates on its axis.

cards.title: Brahmagupta
cards.subtitle: Master of Zero
cards.description: The 7th-century mathematician who, in the Brahmasphutasiddhanta, first set out rules for arithmetic with zero and negative numbers.

cards.title: Bhaskara II
cards.subtitle: Bhaskaracharya
cards.description: The 12th-century mathematician whose Lilavati and Siddhanta Shiromani anticipated ideas of calculus and the derivative centuries before Europe.

cards.title: Bhaskara I
cards.subtitle: The Sine Approximator
cards.description: The 7th-century astronomer, the first to write numbers in the Hindu place-value system with a circle for zero, famous for a remarkably accurate sine formula.

cards.title: Varahamihira
cards.subtitle: The Polymath of Ujjain
cards.description: The 6th-century astronomer-polymath whose Pancha-Siddhantika and Brihat Samhita compiled astronomy, astrology and natural science.

cards.title: Madhava of Sangamagrama
cards.subtitle: Founder of the Kerala School
cards.description: The 14th-century mathematician who discovered infinite series for pi and the trigonometric functions, anticipating calculus by some 250 years.

cards.title: Sushruta
cards.subtitle: The Father of Surgery
cards.description: The ancient surgeon whose Sushruta Samhita describes hundreds of operations and instruments, including rhinoplasty and cataract surgery.

cards.title: Charaka
cards.subtitle: The Father of Medicine
cards.description: The ancient physician whose Charaka Samhita is a foundational text of Ayurveda, covering diagnosis, digestion, immunity and ethics.

cards.title: Kanada
cards.subtitle: The Atom Philosopher
cards.description: The ancient sage who founded the Vaisheshika school and proposed that all matter is made of indivisible particles called anu (atoms).

cards.title: Pingala
cards.subtitle: The First Binary Thinker
cards.description: The ancient scholar whose Chandahshastra used a binary system and arrays that prefigured Pascal's triangle and the Fibonacci sequence.

cards.title: Baudhayana
cards.subtitle: Geometer of the Altars
cards.description: The author of the Sulba Sutras, which stated a form of the Pythagorean theorem and an accurate approximation of the square root of two.

cards.title: Panini
cards.subtitle: The Grammar Machine
cards.description: The ancient grammarian whose Ashtadhyayi described Sanskrit with nearly 4,000 formal rules, a system admired as a forerunner of computer science.

cards.title: Jagadish Chandra Bose
cards.subtitle: Pioneer of Radio & Plant Science
cards.description: The polymath who demonstrated millimetre radio waves and showed that plants respond to stimuli, inventing the crescograph to measure their growth.

cards.title: C. V. Raman
cards.subtitle: The Raman Effect
cards.description: The physicist who discovered the scattering of light that bears his name, winning the 1930 Nobel Prize — the first for an Asian in the sciences.

cards.title: Srinivasa Ramanujan
cards.subtitle: The Man Who Knew Infinity
cards.description: The self-taught mathematical genius who, in a short life, produced thousands of results in number theory, partitions and infinite series.

cards.title: Satyendra Nath Bose
cards.subtitle: Father of the Boson
cards.description: The physicist whose work on quantum statistics, developed with Einstein, gave rise to Bose-Einstein statistics and the particle class called bosons.

cards.title: Meghnad Saha
cards.subtitle: Reader of the Stars
cards.description: The astrophysicist whose Saha ionization equation linked the spectra of stars to their temperature, transforming stellar astrophysics.

cards.title: Homi J. Bhabha
cards.subtitle: Father of the Nuclear Programme
cards.description: The physicist who founded India's atomic energy programme and TIFR, known for Bhabha scattering in particle physics.

cards.title: Vikram Sarabhai
cards.subtitle: Father of the Space Programme
cards.description: The visionary physicist who founded ISRO and the Physical Research Laboratory, launching India into the space age.

cards.title: Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar
cards.subtitle: The Chandrasekhar Limit
cards.description: The astrophysicist who showed that a white dwarf above about 1.4 solar masses must collapse, winning the 1983 Nobel Prize in Physics.

cards.title: Har Gobind Khorana
cards.subtitle: Cracker of the Genetic Code
cards.description: The biochemist who helped decipher how the genetic code directs protein synthesis, sharing the 1968 Nobel Prize in Medicine.

cards.title: Venkatraman Ramakrishnan
cards.subtitle: Mapper of the Ribosome
cards.description: The structural biologist who shared the 2009 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for revealing the atomic structure of the ribosome.

cards.title: Prafulla Chandra Ray
cards.subtitle: Father of Indian Chemistry
cards.description: The chemist who discovered mercurous nitrite and founded Bengal Chemicals, India's first pharmaceutical company.

cards.title: Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar
cards.subtitle: Architect of Indian R&D
cards.description: The chemist who built the network of national laboratories under the CSIR, after whom India's top science prize is named.

cards.title: Birbal Sahni
cards.subtitle: Reader of Ancient Plants
cards.description: The palaeobotanist who studied India's fossil plants and founded the institute of palaeosciences that bears his name.

cards.title: Salim Ali
cards.subtitle: The Birdman of India
cards.description: The ornithologist whose surveys and writings transformed the study of Indian birds and shaped the country's conservation movement.

cards.title: E. K. Janaki Ammal
cards.subtitle: Pioneering Botanist
cards.description: The botanist and cytogeneticist celebrated for her work on sugarcane and plant chromosomes; a magnolia is named in her honour.

cards.title: Anna Mani
cards.subtitle: The Weather Woman of India
cards.description: The physicist and meteorologist who designed instruments to measure solar radiation, ozone and wind, modernising Indian weather science.

cards.title: Kamala Sohonie
cards.subtitle: A Door Opened for Women
cards.description: The biochemist who, against great resistance, became the first Indian woman to earn a PhD in a scientific field, studying nutrition and the drink neera.

cards.title: G. N. Ramachandran
cards.subtitle: The Ramachandran Plot
cards.description: The biophysicist who proposed the triple-helix structure of collagen and devised the Ramachandran plot used to study protein structure.

cards.title: Yellapragada Subbarow
cards.subtitle: Quiet Conqueror of Disease
cards.description: The biochemist whose discoveries underpinned ATP energy chemistry and yielded the drugs methotrexate and synthetic folic acid.

cards.title: M. S. Swaminathan
cards.subtitle: Father of the Green Revolution
cards.description: The agricultural scientist whose high-yield wheat varieties helped India move from famine to food self-sufficiency.

cards.title: Verghese Kurien
cards.subtitle: The Milkman of India
cards.description: The engineer who led Operation Flood and the Amul cooperative model, making India the world's largest milk producer.

cards.title: Raj Reddy
cards.subtitle: A Pioneer of AI
cards.description: The computer scientist and Turing Award laureate who pioneered large-scale speech recognition and artificial intelligence.

cards.title: E. C. George Sudarshan
cards.subtitle: The Overlooked Genius
cards.description: The theoretical physicist behind the V-A theory of the weak force and the quantum theory of optical coherence.

cards.title: Harish-Chandra
cards.subtitle: Architect of Representation Theory
cards.description: The mathematician whose deep work on harmonic analysis and the representation theory of Lie groups shaped modern mathematics.

cards.title: C. R. Rao
cards.subtitle: A Giant of Statistics
cards.description: The statistician whose Cramer-Rao bound and Rao-Blackwell theorem are cornerstones of modern statistical theory.

cards.title: P. C. Mahalanobis
cards.subtitle: Founder of Indian Statistics
cards.description: The scientist who founded the Indian Statistical Institute, devised the Mahalanobis distance and shaped national economic planning.

cards.title: Satish Dhawan
cards.subtitle: Builder of Indian Rocketry
cards.description: The aerospace engineer and ISRO chairman who guided India's launch-vehicle programme; the Sriharikota spaceport is named for him.

cards.title: U. R. Rao
cards.subtitle: The Satellite Man
cards.description: The space scientist who led the building of Aryabhata, India's first satellite, and later chaired ISRO.

cards.title: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam
cards.subtitle: The Missile Man
cards.description: The aerospace engineer who led the SLV-III and India's missile programme before serving as President; a beloved teacher of science.

cards.title: Asima Chatterjee
cards.subtitle: Chemist of Healing Plants
cards.description: The organic chemist renowned for work on alkaloids and anti-epileptic and anti-malarial drugs; the first Indian woman to earn a Doctor of Science.

cards.title: Obaid Siddiqi
cards.subtitle: Founder of Indian Molecular Biology
cards.description: The molecular biologist and neurogeneticist who founded India's molecular biology research and the National Centre for Biological Sciences.

cards.title: Tessy Thomas
cards.subtitle: The Missile Woman
cards.description: The aerospace scientist who became the first woman to lead an Indian missile project, directing the long-range Agni programme.

cards.title: Raja Ramanna
cards.subtitle: The Nuclear Mind
cards.description: The nuclear physicist who led the team behind India's first nuclear test, the 1974 Smiling Buddha at Pokhran.

cards.title: Jayant Narlikar
cards.subtitle: Questioner of the Big Bang
cards.description: The astrophysicist, co-author of the Hoyle-Narlikar theory of gravity, who founded the centre for astronomy and astrophysics at Pune.

cards.title: Narinder Singh Kapany
cards.subtitle: Father of Fibre Optics
cards.description: The physicist whose pioneering work on light transmission through glass fibres founded the field of fibre optics.

cards.title: Kalpana Chawla
cards.subtitle: Among the Stars
cards.description: The aerospace engineer and first woman of Indian origin in space, who died aboard the Space Shuttle Columbia in 2003.

cards.title: Rakesh Sharma
cards.subtitle: The First Indian in Space
cards.description: The air-force pilot who in 1984 became the first Indian citizen in space, famously calling his homeland 'Saare Jahan Se Achha' from orbit.

cards.title: M. K. Vainu Bappu
cards.subtitle: A Light for Indian Astronomy
cards.description: The astronomer who revived modern observational astronomy in India and after whom a great optical telescope is named.

cards.title: ISRO
cards.subtitle: The Indian Space Research Organisation
cards.description: India's national space agency, founded in 1969, known for low-cost launches, lunar and Mars missions, and the PSLV workhorse.

cards.title: DRDO
cards.subtitle: Defence Research & Development
cards.description: India's defence research organisation, founded in 1958, behind missiles, radars, aircraft and strategic systems.

cards.title: Indian Institute of Science
cards.subtitle: The Tata Institute
cards.description: India's premier research institute, founded in 1909 at Bengaluru with the vision of Jamsetji Tata.

cards.title: TIFR
cards.subtitle: Tata Institute of Fundamental Research
cards.description: The Mumbai institute founded by Homi Bhabha in 1945, a leading centre for physics, mathematics and biology.

cards.title: BARC
cards.subtitle: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre
cards.description: India's premier nuclear research facility at Trombay, home to its research reactors and atomic energy programme.

cards.title: CSIR
cards.subtitle: Council of Scientific & Industrial Research
cards.description: The vast network of national laboratories, set up in 1942, that drives India's industrial and applied science research.

cards.title: The IITs
cards.subtitle: Indian Institutes of Technology
cards.description: India's elite engineering and technology institutes, beginning with Kharagpur in 1951, now a global byword for talent.

cards.title: Indian Statistical Institute
cards.subtitle: The Home of Indian Statistics
cards.description: The Kolkata institute founded by P. C. Mahalanobis in 1931, a world centre for statistics and data science.

cards.title: Physical Research Laboratory
cards.subtitle: Cradle of Space Science
cards.description: The Ahmedabad laboratory founded by Vikram Sarabhai in 1947, the birthplace of India's space research.

cards.title: Raman Research Institute
cards.subtitle: Raman's Own Institute
cards.description: The Bengaluru institute founded by C. V. Raman in 1948 to pursue fundamental research in physics.

cards.title: Aryabhata
cards.subtitle: India's First Satellite
cards.description: India's first satellite, built by ISRO and launched in 1975, marking the country's entry into space.

cards.title: Chandrayaan-1
cards.subtitle: India Reaches the Moon
cards.description: India's first lunar mission, launched in 2008, whose instruments confirmed the presence of water on the Moon.

cards.title: Chandrayaan-2
cards.subtitle: Orbiter, Lander and Rover
cards.description: The ambitious 2019 lunar mission whose orbiter still studies the Moon, though its lander did not survive touchdown.

cards.title: Chandrayaan-3
cards.subtitle: Triumph at the South Pole
cards.description: The 2023 mission that made India the first nation to land near the Moon's south pole and the fourth to soft-land at all.

cards.title: Mangalyaan
cards.subtitle: The Mars Orbiter Mission
cards.description: The 2013-14 mission that made India the first country to reach Mars orbit on its very first attempt, and at remarkably low cost.

cards.title: Aditya-L1
cards.subtitle: India's Sun Watcher
cards.description: India's first dedicated solar observatory, launched in 2023 to study the Sun from the L1 Lagrange point.

cards.title: Smiling Buddha
cards.subtitle: India's First Nuclear Test
cards.description: Code name of India's first nuclear test, conducted at Pokhran in 1974.

cards.title: Pokhran-II
cards.subtitle: Operation Shakti
cards.description: The series of nuclear tests at Pokhran in 1998 that established India as a declared nuclear power.

cards.title: The Green Revolution
cards.subtitle: From Famine to Surplus
cards.description: The transformation of Indian agriculture in the 1960s and 70s through high-yield seeds, irrigation and fertilisers.

cards.title: The White Revolution
cards.subtitle: Operation Flood
cards.description: The dairy cooperative movement that made India the world's largest milk producer, led by Verghese Kurien.

cards.title: SLV-3
cards.subtitle: India's First Launch Vehicle
cards.description: India's first satellite launch vehicle, which in 1980 placed the Rohini satellite in orbit under project director A. P. J. Abdul Kalam.

cards.title: 104 Satellites
cards.subtitle: A World Record Launch
cards.description: The 2017 PSLV flight that launched 104 satellites in a single mission, a world record at the time.

cards.title: Gaganyaan
cards.subtitle: India's Crewed Spaceflight
cards.description: India's programme to send astronauts to orbit aboard an indigenous spacecraft, its first human spaceflight effort.

cards.title: Apsara
cards.subtitle: Asia's First Reactor
cards.description: Commissioned at Trombay in 1956, Apsara was the first nuclear research reactor in Asia.

cards.title: Zero
cards.subtitle: The Number That Changed the World
cards.description: The treatment of zero as a number in its own right, formalised in India, which made modern mathematics possible.

cards.title: The Decimal System
cards.subtitle: The Power of Place
cards.description: The base-ten place-value notation, developed in India and carried west as the Hindu-Arabic numerals used worldwide.

cards.title: The Raman Effect
cards.subtitle: Why the Sea is Blue
cards.description: The inelastic scattering of light by molecules, discovered by C. V. Raman, now a key tool for identifying materials.

cards.title: Bose-Einstein Statistics
cards.subtitle: The Physics of Bosons
cards.description: The quantum statistics, founded on S. N. Bose's work, that govern particles called bosons and predict the Bose-Einstein condensate.

cards.title: The Saha Equation
cards.subtitle: The Chemistry of Stars
cards.description: Meghnad Saha's equation linking the ionisation of a gas to its temperature and pressure, the foundation of stellar spectroscopy.

cards.title: The Chandrasekhar Limit
cards.subtitle: When a Star Must Die
cards.description: The maximum mass, about 1.4 times the Sun, that a white dwarf can have before collapsing into a neutron star or black hole.

cards.title: The Number 1729
cards.subtitle: The Taxicab Number
cards.description: The Hardy-Ramanujan number, the smallest expressible as a sum of two cubes in two different ways, made famous by Ramanujan.

cards.title: The Ramachandran Plot
cards.subtitle: The Map of Proteins
cards.description: A diagram devised by G. N. Ramachandran that maps the allowed twists of a protein's backbone, central to structural biology.

cards.title: The Mahalanobis Distance
cards.subtitle: Measuring the Unusual
cards.description: A statistical measure of how far a point lies from a distribution, devised by P. C. Mahalanobis and widely used in data science.

cards.title: Ayurveda
cards.subtitle: The Science of Life
cards.description: One of the world's oldest systems of medicine, built on balance among the doshas and on diet, herbs and lifestyle.

cards.title: The Kerala School
cards.subtitle: Calculus Before Newton
cards.description: The medieval school of mathematics, led by Madhava, that developed infinite series for pi and trigonometric functions.

cards.title: The Sine Function
cards.subtitle: From Jya to Sine
cards.description: The Indian functions jya and kojya, ancestors of the modern sine and cosine, passed through Arabic into world mathematics.

cards.title: UPI
cards.subtitle: A Payments Revolution
cards.description: The Unified Payments Interface, an Indian-built real-time system that turned the country into a world leader in digital payments.

cards.title: Aadhaar
cards.subtitle: The World's Largest ID System
cards.description: India's biometric digital identity programme, the largest of its kind in the world.

cards.title: PSLV
cards.subtitle: The Workhorse of ISRO
cards.description: The Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle, ISRO's reliable rocket that has launched hundreds of satellites for India and the world.

cards.title: GSLV
cards.subtitle: The Cryogenic Rocket
cards.description: India's Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle, which mastered the difficult indigenous cryogenic engine.

cards.title: PARAM 8000
cards.subtitle: India's First Supercomputer
cards.description: The indigenous supercomputer built by C-DAC in 1991 after India was denied imported machines, launching the PARAM series.

cards.title: The Jaipur Foot
cards.subtitle: Mobility for Millions
cards.description: The low-cost, rugged prosthetic limb developed in India that has restored mobility to millions of amputees worldwide.

cards.title: The Crescograph
cards.subtitle: Bose's Plant Recorder
cards.description: Jagadish Chandra Bose's instrument that magnified and recorded the minute growth and responses of plants.

cards.title: BrahMos
cards.subtitle: The Supersonic Cruise Missile
cards.description: The India-Russia supersonic cruise missile, among the fastest of its kind in service.

cards.title: The Agni Missile
cards.subtitle: India's Long Reach
cards.description: DRDO's family of long-range ballistic missiles, a pillar of India's strategic deterrent.

cards.title: Tejas
cards.subtitle: The Light Combat Aircraft
cards.description: India's indigenous lightweight multirole fighter jet, developed over decades and inducted into the air force.

cards.title: Wootz Steel
cards.subtitle: The Steel of Legends
cards.description: The high-carbon crucible steel made in ancient South India, prized across the world and the basis of fabled Damascus blades.

cards.title: The Seamless Celestial Globe
cards.subtitle: A Metallurgical Marvel
cards.description: Hollow metal globes cast in one seamless piece by Mughal-era metalworkers in Kashmir and Sialkot, long thought impossible to make.

cards.title: The Aryabhatiya
cards.subtitle: A Treatise of the Cosmos
cards.description: Aryabhata's compact 5th-century treatise on mathematics and astronomy, written in verse, that influenced scholars for centuries.

cards.title: The Bakhshali Manuscript
cards.subtitle: An Ancient Zero
cards.description: An ancient birch-bark mathematical manuscript whose dotted symbol is among the earliest known uses of zero.
